job_description:
Technical Services Engineer 
London, EC1R (office/field based, with travel to customer sites across London and the South of England) 
Up to £40,000 DOE Plus Pension Scheme, Laptop, Mobile Phone, 20 Days’ Holiday (rising with service)  
  
Backbone is an IT managed service provider that specialises in providing small and medium sized businesses with a comprehensive range of local on-premise and cloud based IT services. 
  
With a growing portfolio of over 175 customers, we operate in a very busy and demanding space where needs change fast and new projects and opportunities are constantly becoming available. 
  
We’re now looking for a Technical Services Engineer to help maintain, develop, secure and improve the services delivered to our Managed Services customers. 
  
As a Technical Services Engineer, you will: 
  
- Manage the lifecycle of local and cloud based infrastructures 
- Work to enhance existing cloud products and services 
- Provide onsite escalation for Service desk activities 
- Identify and propose solutions to customers’ issues 
- Contribute to the ongoing development of technical standards and documents 
- Spend approximately 50% of your role visiting customers to service their needs. 
  
Interested? 
  
To join us as a Technical Services Engineer, you’ll need great communication skills. Experience of the following is also essential: 
  
- Windows OS (Windows 10) 
- TCP/IP Networking (routing and switching in wired and wireless LAN/WAN environments) 
- Microsoft Office 365, Exchange Online and Azure 
- x86 Server hardware 
- MS Office 2016 and common desktop software 
- Fortinet network security hardware 
- VoIP (Skype for Business) 
- Terminal services, Group Policy, registry and user profiles and Active Directory 
- Windows server 2016 
- Windows server builds, file server / DFS, IIS and clustering 
- Internet connectivity 
- Office moves 
- Desktop client refresh 
- Server infrastructure refresh 
  
Knowledge of the following would be beneficial: MaaS360, VMware Horizon DaaS or Horizon View 6+, VMware, vSphere v5.5 and above, scripting or Linux. 
  
We’d love to hear from you if you’ve worked as a Technical Support Engineer, Service Desk Engineer, Helpdesk Engineer, Infrastructure Engineer, Infrastructure Support Analyst, IT Support Analyst, Cloud Support Analyst or IT Support Engineer. 
  
What are the benefits? 
  
- The flexibility to work from home when necessary 
- Become a key member of a small, but rapidly growing team  
- Opportunities to get involved in various areas of the business 
- Get involved with a range of projects 
- Take on a varied and challenging role where no two days are the same  
- Vibrant and engaging work environment  
  
Webrecruit and Backbone are equal opportunities employers, value diversity and are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all employees and all applicants for employment. Equal opportunities are the only acceptable way to conduct business and we believe that the more inclusive our environments are, the better our work will be. 
  
To apply for the role of Technical Services Engineer, please apply via the button shown. This vacancy is being advertised by Webrecruit. The services advertised by Webrecruit are those of an Employment Agency.

MNG Management Limited is a Management Consultancy and BPO Service provider with offices based in Wapping, London. We are currently looking for an experienced Systems Administrator to join our small team to overhaul the existing IT set up to ensure that we offer a first rate level of IT support required by our Clients.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

The System Administrator (SA) is responsible for effective provisioning, installation/configuration, operation, and maintenance of systems hardware and software and related infrastructure. This individual participates in technical research and development to enable continuing innovation within the infrastructure. This individual ensures that system hardware, operating systems, software systems, and related procedures adhere to organizational values.

This individual will assist project teams with technical issues in the Initiation and Planning phases of our standard Project Management Methodology. These activities include the definition of needs, benefits, and technical strategy; research & development within the project life-cycle; technical analysis and design; and support of operations staff in executing, testing and rolling-out the solutions. Participation on projects is focused on smoothing the transition of projects from development staff to production staff by performing operations activities within the project life-cycle.

This individual is accountable for the following systems: Linux and Windows systems that support GIS infrastructure; Linux, Windows and Application systems that support Asset Management; Responsibilities on these systems include SA engineering and provisioning, operations and support, maintenance and research and development to ensure continual innovation.

SA Engineering and Provisioning

1. Engineering of SA-related solutions for various project and operational needs.
2. Install new / rebuild existing servers and configure hardware, peripherals, services, settings, directories, storage, etc. in accordance with standards and project/operational requirements.
3. Install and configure systems such as supports GIS infrastructure applications or Asset Management applications.
4. Develop and maintain installation and configuration procedures.
5. Contribute to and maintain system standards.
6. Research and recommend innovative, and where possible automated approaches for system administration tasks.  Identify approaches that leverage our resources and provide economies of scale.

Operations and Support

7. Perform daily system monitoring, verifying the integrity and availability of all hardware, server resources, systems and key processes, reviewing system and application logs, and verifying completion of scheduled jobs such as backups.
8. Perform regular security monitoring to identify any possible intrusions.
9. Perform daily backup operations, ensuring all required file systems and system data are successfully backed up to the appropriate media, recovery tapes or disks are created, and media is recycled and sent off site as necessary.
10. Perform regular file archival and purge as necessary.
11. Create, change, and delete user accounts per request.
12. Provide Tier III/other support per request from various constituencies.  Investigate and troubleshoot issues.
13. Repair and recover from hardware or software failures.  Coordinate and communicate with impacted constituencies.

Maintenance

14. Apply OS patches and upgrades on a regular basis, and upgrade administrative tools and utilities. Configure / add new services as necessary.
15. Upgrade and configure system software that supports GIS infrastructure applications or Asset Management applications per project or operational needs.
16. Maintain operational, configuration, or other procedures.
17. Perform periodic performance reporting to support capacity planning.
18. Perform ongoing performance tuning, hardware upgrades, and resource optimization as required.  Configure CPU, memory, and disk partitions as required.
19. Maintain data center environmental and monitoring equipment.

KNOWLEDGE/SKILLS: 

1. Bachelor (4-year) degree, with a technical major, such as engineering or computer science.
2. Systems Administration/System Engineer certification in Unix and Microsoft.
3. Four to six year’s system administration experience.

COMPLEXITY/PROBLEM SOLVING:

1. Position deals with a variety of problems and sometime has to decide which answer is best. The question/issues are typically clear and requires determination of which answer (from a few choices) is the best.

RESPONSIBILITY/OVERSIGHT -FINANCIAL & SUPERVISORY:

1. Functions as a lead worker doing the work similar to those in the work unit; responsibility for training, instruction, setting the work pace, and possibly evaluating performance.
2. Budget responsibility.

COMMUNICATIONS/INTERPERSONAL CONTACTS:

1. Interpret and/or discuss information with others, which involves terminology or concepts not familiar to many people; regularly provide advice and recommend actions involving rather complex issues. May resolve problems within established practices.
2. Provides occasional guidance, some of which is technical.

WORKING CONDITIONS/PHYSICAL EFFORT:

1. Responsibilities will require working evenings and weekends, sometimes with little advanced notice.
2. No regular travel required.

Overview

The growing development team at Mountain Warehouse is split into 4 teams, each looking after a different set of applications and using a loose Kanban approach. The architecture is microservices with continuous deployment.

We are a collaborative team with a strong culture of code review, occasional usage of pair programming, lots of unit tests. We’re keen to write code following best practice and to continuously find ways that we can be even better. The work is a mixture of green-field and legacy work (much like any other mature organisation) mostly using C# and SQL Server - the microservices approach allows us to use other technologies as appropriate and we’ve got some MongoDB applications in development.

What you'll be doing

We are looking for developers to join any of our 3 UK teams, the roles are primarily back-end” but there is some user interface work to be done for those with an interest in it.

We would love to meet someone with

-C# in a commercial environment.
-Ability to solve problems in code quickly and simply.
-Solid understanding of how http and network communication works.
-Excellent communication skills.
-Ability to produce initial results rapidly and iterate based upon feedback.

This is a selection of what we’re using - let us know what you have experience of, we can train in the rest.

-Test Driven Development.
-RabbitMQ / MassTransit.
-Microservices architecture.
-Entity Framework
-MVC.
-MongoDB.
-React.
-RESTful API design / Web.API.
-Machine Learning.
-Azure.
-Jenkins.
-Docker.
-.net Core.
-GitFlow. 
-Dependency Injection with AutoFac, Unity and StructureMap.
-SQL Server stored procedures.

We have regular learning days, lunch’n’learns, two hack events a year and a budget for training courses or conferences. The department is light on meetings and big on doing - we give you the time to complete your work to a high standard. As a privately-owned company we tend to be fast-moving, and as we’re profitable and growing we tend to be able to afford the tools and hardware we need.The IT department is over the road from the rest of head office. We have a large, modern open-space environment that’s been recently refurbished.It’s a sociable environment - the current team tend to go for lunch most days, the local pub is occupied by the company most Fridays, there are two big parties a year, etc.

What's in it for you?

-Competitive salary and benefits
-22 days holiday allowance with bank holidays, plus 1 day per year at the company
-50% staff discount & 25% for family and friends
-Training budget & access to training tools and classes
-Private medical cover
-Pension scheme
-Life Insurance (eligible after a probationary period)
-Travel season ticket loan (eligible after a probationary period)
-Cycle to work scheme (eligible after a probationary period)
-Childcare voucher scheme (eligible after a probationary period)
-Free Yoga and Pilates classes on site
-Fresh fruit daily
-Fully subsidised summer and Christmas parties
-Regular sample sales

About Us

At Mountain Warehouse we have been developing the very best outdoor gear for all the family since 1997.

Unlike other outdoor retailers, we don’t carry lots of different brands. In fact, almost all the products we sell through our stores and website are exclusive to Mountain Warehouse - you won’t find them anywhere else.

Why do we do this? Well, by designing our own top quality products and having them made to our exacting standards, we can cut out the middleman. So that means you get the exact same quality, but at significantly lower prices than you’ll find at other shops.

We have well located and well stocked stores where you and your family can get straightforward advice from our knowledgeable, friendly staff. In most of our stores you can even bring your dog in too! Everything we do is focused on serving our growing group of customers who like what we have to offer: the best gear, the best service and the best prices.

We would love it if you joined them.

Data Science Manager
Notting Hill, London
£Competitive salary DOE + Flexible Benefits, Bonus, Flexible Working Hours, 25 Days' Holiday + Day Off on Birthday

Tell me about VCharge….

VCharge is the centrepiece of OVO’s ambition in becoming the world leader in distributed energy technology. It is an intelligent control layer running on top of the already existing energy infrastructure, giving both the market players as well the end consumers a fast forward ticket to the distributed energy future. VCharge will be the nervous system of the OVO’s energy IoT landscape sitting between grid operators and end customers, and connecting energy suppliers, energy IoT and VPP.

Join a team of top-notch tech talent in their quest to shape the energy ecosystem of the future. You will be working shoulder to shoulder with people from Facebook, Amazon, Manor Racing, Goldman Sachs, to name but a few.

That sounds great! What will I be doing?

You’ll manage a small team of data scientists whilst also being hands on as a senior data scientist. You will be interacting with senior stakeholders in the business as well as your peers (product managers and engineering managers) to better understand the problem space and shape the data science agenda. In addition to line management duties, you will need to bring best practices and processes to the team and help each of the team members maximise their potential. You will help define what success looks like for the team and define clear team and personal objectives for each of the team members.

You’ll apply your expertise in quantitative analysis, data mining, and the presentation of data to see beyond the numbers and understand how our users interact with our core/business products.

That sounds super exciting, but… Do I have what it takes?

- Extensive experience as a data scientist
- Experience in a hands-on managerial role in a collaborative data science team.
- Experience in physical simulation, control systems, time series modelling and forecasting. statistical learning. Experience with complex data set visualisations.
- Solid understanding of statistics, algorithmics, optimisation and modelling techniques.
- Experience working with senior stakeholders, laser focus on delivery. Experience working closely with product managers and engineering managers.
- Experience in setting tools/processes/frameworks to facilitate the work of the data science team. Big focus on communication and Cross-sector/industry experience.
- Used to working with large volumes of data, and is knowledgeable of the various tools to manipulate these as well as various data manipulation and visualisation tools. Experience with data science cloud platform technologies (e.g. AWS/GCP).
- Led successful data science projects from inception, design, implementation, testing, deployment, visualisation and stakeholder presentation.
- Ability to initiate and drive projects to completion with minimal guidance. Ability to visualise, communicate and explain complicated concepts to a non-technical crowd.
- Fluency in SQL and Python - Go/Scala would be a plus.
- Be up to date on the latest trends in the data science/data engineering community. 

Benefits

- Sunday Times' top 20 list of the best companies to work for
-4% on top of salary for flexible benefits
- The opportunity to get involved with conferences, meet-ups, hackathons, tech talks and teaching kids to code
- A clearly defined career path
- Open plan office in Notting Hill
- Free breakfast every day and lunch on Fridays
- Lots of social events
- Training budget
- Matched volunteering and matched fundraising

To apply for the role of Data Science Manager, please apply via the button shown.

This vacancy is being advertised by Webrecruit. The services advertised by Webrecruit are those of an Employment Agency.

Additional Keywords: Data Science Manager, Senior Data Scientist, Lead Data Scientist, Data Science Manager, Data Insight Manager, Senior Data Insight Analyst, Data Analysis Manager or Data Science Specialist.

MI5’s Infrastructure Services is made up of a number of engineering teams, each playing their part to ensure the organisation’s IT Services are reliable, stable and resilient to help keep the country safe. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of one of these teams and contribute to protecting the UK from threats to national security, including terrorism and espionage.

We are seeking individuals who have a passion for IT, are enthusiastic and keen to learn. You will already have some IT experience in any of the roles on offer, and regardless of which area you join us in, we will develop your technical capability with on the job training and an opportunity to obtain industry recognised accreditation where necessary.

On this occasion, we are looking individuals to join the following areas within the Infrastructure Services:

•Network Engineers
•Desktop Implementation Engineers
•Infrastructure Tools Engineers
•Mobile Connections Engineers
•Data Centre Service Engineers
•IT Operations Engineers
•Systems Monitoring Engineer

 Key Responsibilities 

Depending on the role, your responsibilities will vary but you can be sure you will be part of a supportive team and provided with the opportunity to develop your technical knowledge for a rewarding career within MI5. This is not an exhaustive list of responsibilities

•Support and troubleshoot a variety of different IT systems;
•Build a good customer relationship with end users and deliver excellent customer service;
•Receive and respond to IT incidents and service requests within agreed SLAs;
•Liaise regularly with other IT support teams, including partners in other UK intelligence agencies; to ensure they are up to date and informed;
•Identify problems and areas for continual service improvement;

 The Person 

We are looking for enthusiastic individuals who have a strong desire to develop their technical skills in a specific area of MI5’s infrastructure Services. You will be expected to work as part of a team, dealing directly with customers and sometimes in response to time critical operational requests. Therefore, strong troubleshooting ability is essential for success in this role.

You will be required to be able to effectively plan ahead and set clearly defined and realistic deadlines, taking a flexible approach to problem solving.

As we are seeking a wide range of technical skills, we are seeking individuals who can demonstrate knowledge and experience in some of these areas: a good working knowledge of Microsoft (MS) operating systems, including MS office suite. Practical experience in IT installation and PC hardware diagnosis and repair; a good knowledge of Networking, Windows SCCM 2012/CB, Windows Server Management and Mobile Communications; a good understanding of network, storage and computer hardware platforms as well as Data Centre Infrastructure Management (DCIM) and experience in using Data Centre toolsets.

ITIL foundation is desirable, but not essential.

 Key skills 

We are also looking for people with some experience in any of the following areas

•Proxy Configuration
•Hardware installation and diagnosis
•VB Script or PowerShell
•Microsoft Windows operating system support
•Microsoft Office Support
•RHEL Administration 
•Enterprise Anti-Virus support

We particularly welcome applications from women and Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic candidates. 

 To find out more and apply, please visit our website by clicking the APPLY button. 

To apply to MI5 you must be a born or naturalised British citizen, over 18 years old and normally have lived in the UK for nine of the last ten years. You should not discuss your application, other than with your partner or close family member

You will play a key role in development support, enhancement and build-out of core applications within our Investment Bank technology portfolio. You will develop leading edge technologies in collaboration with a truly globally distributed development team. As part of Investment Bank Technology you’ll be part of a major innovation project, improving award winning platforms and enhancing the way people manage trades and investments.

If you have strong experience developing HTML5 applications, with exposure and understanding of front and back-end languages/technologies. And you want to find new applications for your code, making a big impact on a global scale, then Barclays is the place for you. We’re always developing - you should be too.
 
Your Role
• The role is 100% hands-on with 80% development and 20% support
• You will develop high quality maintainable code
• You will make technical decisions that require a strong understanding of the problem domain
• You will collaborate and communicate globally with other developers to build technical solutions
• You will have a strong knowledge of software development methods, tools and standards and actively keep this knowledge current (i.e. desire to learn)
• You will adhere to Policies & Standards appropriate to the role - outlined in the Code of Conduct and mandatory training. 
 
Essential Skills
• Proven experience developing medium to large size HTML5 application covering: TypeScript and JavaScript, Angular2+, Bootstrap, CSS3, Less, General server side development and SQL
• Experience developing application in Core Java for general server side development
• Proven Agile experience (preferably Scrum/Kanban)

Desirable Skills
• Knowledge of Equity Derivatives/Structured products and HTTP protocol and web services
• Experience providing support and resolving production issues and cross-region teams and projects
• Experience with HighCharts, Spring Framework, and writing Oracle PL/SQL code
• Experience building systems in trading, banking and/ or financial organization

About Us
Barclays is a transatlantic consumer, corporate and investment bank offering products and services across personal, corporate and investment banking, credit cards and wealth management, with a strong presence in our two home markets of the UK and the US. Our goal is to become the bank of choice by providing superior services to customers and clients and supporting our stakeholders via a commercially successful business that generates long-term sustainable returns.
 
About Barclays International Commercial Growth Initiative 
The Barclays International Markets business is making a major technology investment and has mobilised the Commercial Growth Programme to drive an ambitious revenue growth plan focused on maintaining and building our trading capabilities. This initiative will see a major boost to Technology resource talent who will work on a wide range of leading edge technology programmes. We have identified over 600 roles to be hired across Pre-Trade Technology, Post Trade Technology and Risk, Finance and Treasury Technology globally, including key hubs in London, New York, Glasgow, Pune and Prague. We are hiring creative and driven Technologists, from a wide range of industry sectors that have exposure to high availability and high transactional online systems. There has never been a more exciting time to join Barclays International.

Graduate Opportunities in IT

As a Ford IT graduate you will have the opportunity to move between Ford Motor Company and Ford Credit Europe Bank, our financial subsidiary. This exposure allows you to understand the fundamental operations that drive and support our business.

The distance between imagination and…creation. It can be measured in years of innovation, or in moments of brilliance, and you can start travelling right now. When you join Ford, your journey begins. You become part of a team that is already leading the way, with ingenious solutions and attainable products - and it is always ready to go further.

To be part of our future, you will need: 

-A minimum 2:2 undergraduate degree or a masters or a PhD in any subject, but preferably an IT or IT/business degree
-Experience working with IT solutions
-Effective interpersonal skills and a strong team working ethos
-An ability to communicate clearly in writing, over the phone, using social media and face-to-face
-To be highly motivated and have a dedicated approach from day one
-An innovative flair and ability to creatively problem-solve
-A strong customer focus

You will have the potential to thrive in a dynamic, innovative environment, whilst building positive relationships and collaborating with IT and business colleagues from all over the world to help develop, implement and enhance our information technology solutions. 

Our IT organisation is uniquely placed to anticipate and respond to the multiple key priorities of our automotive and mobility business. You will progress through a series of on-the-job placements to help you build on the knowledge and skills gained during your degree, performing a real role with real responsibilities. Your initial focus is likely to be in one of the following key business areas: 

What to expect:
 
Solutions Development:
- Working with internal business customers to develop, enhance and maintain IT solutions that are critical to the efficient operation of our global business.

Project Management: 
- Developing project charters and plans. Working with stakeholders to manage project budgets, resources and issues/risks, in order to ensure the successful delivery of IT solutions that meet the business critical requirements.

Solutions Support: 
- Providing front-line support for our critical IT applications and services.

Infrastructure Operations:
- Planning, building and operating a global IT infrastructure base.

Security:
- Providing 24x7 protection for our vehicles and critical IT systems to mitigate security breaches, including cyber threats.
 
You will also have the opportunity to develop skills in a variety of areas, such as: software engineering, mobile app development, product design, product management, web development, develops, service operations, strategy and planning, business partnering, and more. 

We will support you in developing strong leadership and communication skills through interaction with global teams, as well as honing your analytical skills finding and implementing innovative solutions to our hardware/software environments. 
Whatever your degree or experience, we will provide you with a wide view of IT in a business context.

Throughout your graduate career and beyond, you will find further opportunities to develop specific technical skills and focus on key leadership competencies. 
Many of our roles offer exciting opportunities to contribute at an international level across our organisation. IT is a global team, so you may also have the opportunity to travel to any Ford location worldwide. Graduates should, however, expect to be based in one of our main UK locations in South East Essex (Warley, Dunton, and Dagenham) or London (Stratford).

At Ford, the distance between you and an amazing career has never been shorter. Join the Ford team today, and discover the benefits, rewards and development opportunities you would expect from a diverse global leader.

Role overview:

A global organisation in Industry is searching for a Senior Internal Auditor to join the team. The purpose of this role is to contribute to successful completion of Audit Plan. Working in a team (with the opportunity to lead) this is an extremely fast paced role that will provide variety, exposure and the room for growth.

 

Responsibilities:

 * Contributes to successful completion of Audit Plan through assistance on audit engagements inclusive of planning and wrap-up activities.
 * Prepares clear and concise audit work papers and audit reports applying standard
 * Willingness to learn and capacity to apply company policies and procedures.
 * Completion of SOX procedures including walkthroughs and testing of critical cycles.
 * Reports to one or more member(s) of the Internal Audit management team.
 * Communicates with audit management and staff including participation in opening and closing meetings.
 * Applies risk and control concepts, identifying and communicating any potential issues in a timely and effective manner.
 * Works to achieve developmental goals set by Manager and actively participates in study programs.
 * Cooperates with all members of the Internal Audit team to complete global projects.
 * Provides value added ideas to improve the organisation.

 

Your Profile:

 * University Degree
 * Accounting/ Auditing experience
 * CPA/ACA, part-qualified or willing to pursue an accounting qualification
 * Experience with SOx
 * Strong analytical and technical skills
 * Knowledge of risk management/ audit principles and an understanding of audit methods and techniques
 * Proficient in Microsoft Office

 

They can offer:

 * Excellent training and development opportunities
 * Exposure across the business
 * Exciting changing and fast paced environment
 * Apply immediately to be considered for this opportunity
 * Competitive salaries and regular reviews

 

 

Apply today to be considered for this exciting opportunity!

Our client is searching for a Hardware Design Engineer with experience in Circuit Design, Schematic Capture and Embedded Electronics to join an exciting team, working on cutting edge products to perfectly meet the requirements of their clientele and customers. 

The role would suit an engineer that has proven experience in the following areas: 

PCB design 

Schematic Capture

Analogue and Digital Design

This role as a Hardware Design Engineer offers a highly competitive salary and is ideal for any candidate that is self-motivated, exhibits a flexible attitude towards prioritisation and seeks to broaden their experience whilst building on existing skill sets. 

For more information or to apply, please contact Daniel Evans.

Product Owner - Gaming/Gambling Space - Permanent - London

My client are an international sports betting and gaming operator, with a market leading presence throughout the UK, Europe, Australia and the US. They are online led, mobile led, and sports led, and their unique products and innovative marketing techniques provide an outstanding experience for their millions of customers. 

What you will be doing..

You will be responsible for setting, prioritising and evaluating the work generated by a Data Scrum team to ensure impeccable features and functionality of the product. You will be working closely with the Head of Data Engagement and other Data Product Owners who are the primary points of contact for their business stakeholders. This role will collaborate closely with Marketing and Product Analytics units to understand data requirements and promote opportunities to leverage data across the organisation. 

What you will have:

 * Experience in data solution management, throughout the entire development and software delivery life cycle
 * Experience working with SCRUM/Agile delivery methods
 * Knowledge of techniques for requirements capture and specification, the ability to balance tactical and strategic needs and always focus on the stakeholder and customer
 * Experience working with Data Architects to produce high quality artefacts as part of the delivery process
 * Data industry understanding and interest with exposure to big data solutions

If you'd be interested in finding out more, please reply with a copy of your CV and I will be in touch.

Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor

West London

Salary: £350 - £400 per day

Job Ref: JR670211

Contract: 6 - 12 months

A specialist M&E Contractor who work primarily on heavy M&E projects such as data centres, energy centres, pharmaceutical, and heavy industrial. They are looking for a Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor to join the existing team.

The successful Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor will to work initially on an energy centre project which is currently live and then to move on to other mission critical projects such as a Data Centre. You will be based from the office in West London, with travel to sites across London and the South East.

What the job of the Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor will involve:

 * You will be responsible for cost controlling on all projects and ensuring budgets are achieved.
 * As M&E Quantity Surveyoryou will be tasked with managing and forecasting the Cash flow of a series of projects.
 * Responsible for valuation and cost control of the building services, sometimes involved in negotiation of contract sum, complete with preparation and negotiation of financial impact on base build works and preliminary costs.
 * Cost planning through to production of tender pricing schedules and producing a pricing schedule and tender analysis.
 * Helping train and mentor a Junior Quantity Surveyor from time to time.

The Requirements of the Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor:

The ideal Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor should:

 * Have a minimum of 5 years' experience working as an MEP Quantity Surveyor within UK construction
 * Must either be MEP bias or offer a wealth of experience on MEP systems
 * Recent experience on heavy MEP projects is an essential - Preferably gained on data centre, energy centre, pharmaceutical lab, clean room, or manufacturing plants / heavy industrial projects

To Apply:

To apply for the position of Senior MEP Quantity Surveyor, or to find out what other opportunities we can offer in the data centre construction sector, please contact Joe Raymond at Project Resource.

Services advertised by Project Resource are that of an Employment Agency/Business.

job_description: Mid/SeniorAndroid Developer- West London - £45,000 to £70,000 + Excellent Benefits Do you want to be involved in digitising the retailjourney with some of the best technologies available?Do you want to be involved with a company that puts work/life balance and a relaxed yet focused vibe first?Do you want to be part of a small, flexible and very friendly team, where input is appreciated, and self-improvement is encouraged?Yes? Well I look forward to hearing from you and hope you enjoy reading on...My client area technology and data company that uses the language of games on a fleet of interactive screens, called PlaySpots, to transform the way retailers and brands interact with shoppers to engage, research and monetize their footfall, linking offline behaviours with online profiles.As retailers digitise their physical locations at an unprecedented rate, my client is able to create a breakthrough model by combining scalable engagement, machine learning and IoT for passive and active data.Their London and Singapore offices share a relaxed yet focused vibe where teams dare to think big, care -almost painfully- about the quality of what they are providing to other team members and clients, and focus on keeping things simple, removing any unnecessary hurdle, in an effort to wow all those around them.About you...As an Android engineer working for my client, you will be working on low level Android features to complex user interfaces alike. The fact my client has their own platform allows them to separate the codebase into many applications to simplify development and testing. job_title: Software Tester - Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant
job_description:
Software Tester / Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant who enjoys working with the latest technologies on a diverse range of projects, that also has experience developing test frameworks and building robust, scalable and effective automated tests is required for a well-established and successful Global Company.


PLEASE NOTE: Travel to client sites throughout the UK will also be required with overnight stays


SALARY: Negotiable Depending on Experience

BENEFITS INCLUDE:

•	A supported professional Training and Testing & QA Certification Programme

•	Defined Career and Progression Plan

•	Competitive Package + Overtime + Training + Healthcare + 7% Pension

•	After an initial period - eligibility to apply for the staff mobility programme with Australia, New Zealand and India

•	The opportunity to help build the Company’s test practice from near the start and really contribute to the development of the corporate culture


LOCATION: London initially with regular visits to the Head Office in Reading, Berkshire. 

PLEASE NOTE: Travel to client sites throughout the UK will also be required with overnight stays


** Exceptional Career Development Opportunities **

** Diverse Range of Projects ** 

** Clear Progression Path **

** Work for a Global, Dynamic, Market Leader **


IS THIS YOU?

•	Do you want to work with the latest technologies?

•	Do you want to work for a Company that invests in your personal and professional development and can offer excellent career prospects?

•	Do you have experience working as an Automation Tester? 

•	Would you like to work on a diverse range of projects?

•	Would you like the job security that comes with working at a global leading company?

If you can answer ‘YES’ to these questions then we want to hear from you!


IMPORTANT - PLEASE READ: All Applicants MUST HAVE full working rights for the UK, be located within the UK right now, or if not be able to be on board within the UK within a 4 week period


JOB OVERVIEW

We have a fantastic new job opportunity for a Software Tester / Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant who enjoys working with the latest technologies on a diverse range of projects, that also has experience developing test frameworks and building robust, scalable and effective automated tests.

Working as the Software Tester / Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant you will consult with the Company’s top tier client base, where you will be required to develop and deliver Test Automation solutions to the highest level of quality.

As the Software Tester / Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant you must have experience working with multiple tools such as Selenium, RFT, MS Coded UI, Ranorex, Tosca, UFT/QTP etc.

The Company has its Head Office based in Reading. However, as they are a consultancy there will be travel required to various client sites across the UK and you will be expected to be prepared to stay away from home during the working week. You must be based in the UK, have full working rights and be ready to go with no delays… 


ABOUT THE COMPANY

As a successful candidate, you will have a great opportunity to join this well-established, market leading Company that employs over 1,500+ permanent employees globally and are currently experiencing rapid growth. Due to this rapid expansion, they are seeking highly experienced Testers to join their UK teams on a permanent basis. You will be working with the best in the industry and enjoying a variety of employee incentives including award-winning career development programmes.

When joining the Company, you will work with like-minded colleagues who have a passion for testing excellence. With an encouraging and supportive environment, the UK team-orientated culture is helpful, friendly, inclusive and flexible, and by no means least, fun! 

The Company operates with a flat-structure feel which enables you to have your contributions heard and acknowledged. They keep up-to-date with the ever-changing IT tools and throughout your career offer you the opportunity to focus on advancing your internationally recognised testing qualification (ISTQB / Agile + more) as well as learn new tools. In addition, they offer staff parties/activities, unrivalled employee benefits and a results-focussed culture that is inclusive, successful and they do it all with positivity and a smile.


APPLY TODAY

If this job looks like your ideal position then please send in your CV as soon as possible for our Recruitment Team to review.


DUTIES

Your duties and responsibilities as the Software Tester / Automation Test Engineer / Manager / Team Leader / Consultant include:

•	Create multiple test plans to cover all test cycles for a particular project

•	Create or assist in the creation of a test strategy

•	Provide estimates of required resources (time, people, skills, environments, tools etc.) for the project at the request of the test manager

•	Identify appropriate resourcing for projects and test phases

•	Validate team estimates

•	Negotiate access and organise configuration/build of suitable test environments

•	Produce test schedules for a phase of testing

•	Implementation and usage of multiple tools and best practices

•	Identify and solve technical challenges with implementation and usage of tools within a small team

•	Able to manage the defect process ensuring that all defects are analysed and fixes are scheduled and retested

•	Provide regular reports to project test management and other interested parties

•	Determine correct test data requirements and manage accordingly

•	Contribute to post implementation reviews for discrete areas

•	Complete client engagements from outset to completion in line with Project Plans and Estimates


IDEAL CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

•	Proven Lead/Management experience within Automation, creating, developing and enhancing automation frameworks from scratch

•	Proven experience delivering quality test automation solutions with multiple tools such as Selenium, RFT, MS Coded UI, Ranorex, Tosca, UFT/QTP etc.

•	BDD with tools such as Cucumber, JBehave and RSpec

•	A solid understanding of OOP development practices with Java, C# or similar

•	A good understanding of web technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript and AJAX

•	Demonstrated ability to solve technical problems and implement innovative solutions

•	Excellent relationship building and communication skills

•	All Applicants MUST HAVE full working rights for the UK, be located within the UK right now, or if not be able to be on board within the UK within a 4 week period


DESIRABLE SKILLS

•	Web Service and API testing

•	Ability to source control your test assets

•	Mobile Testing

•	Practical SQL and database experience

•	Exposure to Agile practices, Continuous Integration tools & DevOps


HOW TO APPLY

To be considered for this job vacancy, please submit your CV to our Recruitment Team who will review your details. CV’s of Job Applicants meeting this requirement will be submitted to our Client for consideration. By submitting your job application to us you are hereby giving us your express consent to submit your details to our Client for this purpose.

PLEASE NOTE: CVs MUST be attached to your email job application in Microsoft Word or PDF format for our systems to process your application correctly, rather than your CV copied and pasted into the body of the email.

JOB REF: AWDO-P4508

Senior Full Stack Software Engineer - Permanent - London

My client are a leading name within sports betting technology and trading. Having a presence in two different divisions, the company employ almost 300 staff, who are all predominantly based at their London HQ.

They are looking for a Full Stack Software Engineer to contribute to the success of a broad range of products as part of their on going application development in the B2C channels development team. Their products are web based, deployed to both public and private clouds and are written mostly in C#, but they also contain a variety of open source technologies including MongoDB, Postgres, Aerospike, ElasticSearch and RabbitMQ.

They are looking for developers who are keen to learn, get exposure to a wide range of tech, want to work on an industry leading platform and have a passion for writing quality testable code and enjoy working in a continuous delivery environment. You will be joining a team of highly regarded engineers, and you will thrive on getting involved across the full stack of tech. 

The successful candidate will develop and maintain software for a variety of business functions, ranging from high traffic high transaction customer facing websites across desktop and mobile, and a highly functional line of business applications, and middleware integration's across all tiers. 

What you will need:

 * Strong web development with HTML 5, CSS2/3, object oriented JavaScript and design patterns
 * Practical understanding of UI design patterns 
 * Experience with REST-based services and JSON
 * Experience working with responsive mobile web applications
 * High competency in developing service oriented software in .NET 4.0 or higher with C#
 * Strong C# technical skills
 * Good knowledge of CMS with time critical data that changes frequently on a high traffic website

If you'd be interested to find out more, please don't hesitate to apply with a copy of your CV and I will be in touch.

Software Engineer - Integration - London

My client are a leading name within sports betting technology and trading. Having a presence in two different divisions, the company employ almost 300 staff, who are all predominantly based at their London HQ. They have market leading skills in data management, modelling, pricing and sports trading. 

You will be working on 'Connect', their award winning cloud based sports pricing platform which allows customers to take automated pre and in-play pricing, and manually use our real time quantitative models over the web to predict the outcome of a range of sports events, delivered in real time through a RESTful based API and streamed via AMQP and rich web application.

This position is a technical role which requires an excellent understanding of how things work, they aren't looking for a coder but a software engineer. You will be responsible for building a new .Net Core solution for low latency price processing. As well as improving the architecture of existing components and update their deployment process to be container based. You will also build out example code and applications which showcase the best integration principles to customers who are new to the system.

What they are looking for:

 * Someone who enjoys responsibility and solving problems. Willing to go the extra mile to get things done and are passionate about what they do
 * Solid understanding and demonstrable experience of C#/.net/.net core deployment (if you're at home in other mainstream languages but like the sound of what they're doing, get in touch)
 * Willing to learn, try new approaches and engage with the team who will challenge your skills

It would be a bonus if you had:

 * Understanding of Actor patterns
 * Understanding RabbitMQ/AMQP
 * TDD
 * Understanding of concurrency and distributed systems
 * Understand the concepts of REST and HATEOS
 * Experience with Git and Github
 * A passion for sport 
 * Knowledge of scripting languages i.e. Python/Powershell

INTERNAL AUDIT MANAGER
 WEST LONDON

£68,000 + PACKAGE

 

One of the most globally recognised brands within the travel and leisure industry, based in West London is looking for an INTERNAL AUDIT MANAGER to join the team to oversee and manage financial, operational (process) and field audits.

 

As the IT Audit Manager, you will have a variety of exciting responsibilities enabling you to oversee audits across the Group. From auditing processes, implementation projects, a wide range of technologies, presenting findings and forging strong relationships you will be managing the end to end audit delivery across the portfolio. This is a demanding role with significant interaction with management and a great opportunity to develop your knowledge of effective business controls that make a valuable contribution to the company's success and profitability.

 

Your Responsibilities:

 * Simultaneously lead multiple teams providing assurance services via financial, operational, compliance and Sarbanes Oxley 404 audits as well as advisory services such as process improvement reviews and strategic initiatives.
 * As appropriate, lead the investigation of allegations in conjunction with the Company's Standards of Business Conduct.
 * For projects assigned, ensure the development of a risk-based audit scope and appropriate audit procedures to be performed.
 * As required, review the work of the team members assigned to projects that you lead.
 * As necessary, document and assess the design of controls under review.
 * Perform audit testing to assess operating effectiveness of controls in place, prepare the appropriate work paper documentation, and conclude as required.
 * Serve as a key interface with management associated with assigned audits.
 * As necessary, lead the discussion of audit issues with management and co-develop action plans to address them timely.
 * Review the audit report in conjunction with assigned audits, ensuring that it is complete, accurate, objective, clear, concise, constructive, and timely.
 * Actively participate in the recruitment of top talent.
 * Coach and provide formal feedback to junior members of the department, including line manager of up to four reports.
 * Build and maintain strong and productive relationships with management and third parties.
 * Assist in driving the annual and ongoing risk assessment processes.
 * Innovate as relates to the department's service delivery methodology.
 * Ensure departmental resources are fully utilized.

 

Your Skills:

 * Extensive auditing experience.

 * Relevant qualified

 * Ideal but not essential - experience with a large, publicly-held, high transaction volume business or Big 4 accounting firm.

 * Demonstrated strong analytical and technical skills that yield meaningful insight.
 * Demonstrated leadership skills.
 * Ability to communicate with impact with all levels of the organization, both written and oral.

 * Ability to apply a strong business acumen to add value and operate with appropriate perspective.
 * Demonstrated ability to cultivate and build strong relationships with key internal customers and third parties.
 * Ability to act as a culture creator within the function.
 * Willingness and ability to effectively and efficiently operate in a fast-paced, change-driven environment.
 * Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite.

job_title: Duty Manager - Customer Service Helpdesk / Incident Management
job_description:
Duty Manager - Customer Service Helpdesk / Incident Management

We are actively looking to secure a Duty Manager to join Experis as one of our expert consultants, delivering services to our clients.

Experis Consultancy is a Global entity with a well-established team with over 1000 consultants on assignment across 20 clients globally. Our UK operation is growing and has very aggressive plans for expansion over the coming years. We form part of the Manpower group of companies that turn over $20 billion a year collectively.

Experis UK have partnerships with major clients across the UK spanning multiple industries; our approach is a very personal one, with both our clients and our own employees. We are passionate about training, technology and career development.

The Role

You will work on the customer service helpdesk proactively monitoring the incident queue for all tickets created automatically (through the monitoring/ticketing systems) or reactively (via telephone, email and online tickets) to ensure that incidents are assigned to the agents with the correct skill set. You will also ensure resolution within customer agreed target times or in accordance to their criticality, importance, priority, and severity.

You may be assigned to work on the 3 following functions as required:

 1 Jeopardy Management
 2 Incident Management
 3 Shift Leader

 

Jeopardy Management Function

 * Act as Incident Manager on Crises incidents
 * Answer phone calls escalations from customers / account team based on ticket Severity/SLA
 * Evaluate Customer Business Impact
 * Work with the agents to make real progress toward resolution with these tickets
 * Once on Duty is escalated should keep the customer informed until the incident is totally resolved

 

Main Duties for Incident Management

 * Progress review - Ensure tickets are being resolved/worked accordingly
 * Reduce Workload through faster processing and cleaner queues
 * Monitor all ticket queues and route/assign incidents to specific teams (Monitor, Resolving, 3rd Level, etc.) to ensure the most efficient and effective resolution result to meet the client´s needs and contract, in accordance to the available resources
 * Regularly check the running tickets and help the agents to prioritise
 * Keep process and customer documentation updated and organised
 * Do regular ticket analysis - check the resolution, the process compliance and the communication quality

 

Shift Leader

 * Help Team Managers by being the eyes of the operation
 * Coach the agents based on the analysis
 * Give input to the development plan of the agents
 * Resolve or escalate problems and service requests according to established procedures
 * Actively cooperate with service improvement plans

Role: Domain Architect  
Location: Osterley  
Start Date: ASAP
 Duration: 3-6 months 

In the position of Domain Architect you will be accountable for creating and supporting the delivery of a data driven architecture that meets the needs of analytical, business and engineering teams. You'll be aligned to a Domain (e.g., Viewing , Digital or Trading) and are accountable for defining their target architecture for the use of data and analytics, defining the roadmap and delivering the architecture that moves that Domain forward through the use of data and analytics. 

As a member of the Data, Technology & Operations team you'll: 

* Own the definition and achievement of target architecture and transitional architectures from the current architecture for data, analytics and data products in the Domain. Gain buy in from all stakeholders for those architectures. 
* Translate the user stories, functional and non-functional requirements into transitional designs, and create an effective data and applications architecture. This will include working closely with the analytical teams to ensure any analytical requirements are met, and business teams to ensure requirements are met and that you drive requirements from an understanding of the business value that can be derived from new data and new uses of data. The analysis and design deliverables will be both paper deliverables and prototypes 
* Identify, create and apply design patterns. 
* Ensure the development and delivery of cost effective Domains by providing a high quality Domain architecture for projects and ensuring that appropriate trade-offs are being made. 
* Work closely with the development teams to ensure that the Domain is well understood and developed in line with the Domain architecture. 
* Be part of the programme and project team and understand the wider deliverables and benefits, if DTO is one of several parties involved. 
* Develop key relationships across project, analyst and business teams to enable the seamless delivery of the Domain. 
* Be the point of contact for the Domain's business and analyst community with regards to the provisioning and processing of data to meet the Domain's business objectives. 
* As a member of the Domain Architecture community, lead the Architecture Board, Peer Review process and other relevant technical communities to ensure that the architecture meets standards, develops new standards where necessary and knowledge is shared. 
* Be able to work within and across Domains in line with project priorities. 
* Lead requirements gathering and solution design to ensure we have effective and well described architectures. Be flexible in ways of working to deliver value at speed whilst makes sure the design meets today's and future needs. For example, the use of Design Thinking, the development of prototypes and being a member of a squad that delivers using Scrum. 

Candidate Specification  

* Expertise in information management, data engineering and customer centric decision making and insight Domains for large scale data complex organisations, including data warehouses and near real time analytical processes. 
* Expertise in data and analytics capability, and the use cases, requirements, architecture & design to deliver that capability at an enterprise scale. Expertise in: 
o The storage and processing of data in Google Cloud Platform, 
o Inmon and Kimball approaches to data modelling and schemas, 
o Data integration / extraction, transformation and load (ETL) of data. 
* Leadership of teams that include Business Analysts and Architects. 
* Experience of working in a matrix team to deliver projects whilst demonstrating accountability for the design 
* Has people management experience in a matrix organisation 
* Experience and prior accountability for requirements through to design, and experience in taking responsibility for the design meeting business functional and non-functional requirements 
* Experience of Cloud and Big Data technologies including the following would be advantageous: Google Cloud Platform, Apache Beam, Python, Java, SQL, Tableau, Netezza, and Sybase IQ. 
* The Domain Architect will be able to deliver and lead processes that are appropriate for the project delivery, including but not limited to leading Design Thinking, workshops, requirements gathering and validation processes, and delivering high level designs. 
* Experience of Data Platforms hosted on Cloud platforms. 
* Experience with CRM, telephony, web technologies, digital systems or video streaming technologies 
* Experience of developing long term customer relationships with business and analytics communities. 
* An expert understanding of the 'art of the possible' from a data and analytics perspective to enable consulting with business and other users about capabilities that can be delivered with data and platforms. 
* An expert understanding of the tools and technologies, processes and procedures that bring data 'to life' 
* Articulate, with excellent written and verbal communication, and strong presentation skills, able to produce high quality, well-structured documentation. 

If you are successful in your application for this role, your appointment will be subject to receiving a positive outcome from your Criminal Record Check.

We are looking for an IT&S Support Engineer to join us and provide 1st/2nd line support at the Francis Crick Institute. The successful candidate should have previous experience in an IT helpdesk environment and be a quick learner. Strong organisational and customer service skills are also needed.

This role is fixed-term for 12 months.

Description 

The Crick has required a state-of-the-art IT&S infrastructure to enable world-leading scientific research. The Crick’s IT&S is science'focused, flexible, and forward-looking, and it expedites collaboration within and beyond the Institute. Research at The Crick is data-intensive, and involves experimental, theoretical and computational science. 
The IT&S Support Engineer will report to the IT&S Helpdesk Manager, providing 1st and 2nd line IT&S support, with primary focus on resolving issues remotely using existing technologies and when necessary by desk visit.

About us

The Francis Crick Institute is a biomedical discovery institute dedicated to understanding the fundamental biology underlying health and disease. Its work is helping to understand why disease develops and to translate discoveries into new ways to prevent, diagnose and treat illnesses such as cancer, heart disease, stroke, infections, and neurodegenerative diseases.
An independent organisation, its founding partners are the Medical Research Council (MRC), Cancer Research UK, Wellcome, UCL (University College London), Imperial College London and King’s College London.
The Crick was formed in 2015, and in 2016 it moved into a new state-of-the-art building in central London which brings together 1500 scientists and support staff working collaboratively across disciplines, making it the biggest biomedical research facility under in one building in Europe.
The Francis Crick Institute will be world-class with a strong national role. Its distinctive vision for excellence includes commitments to collaboration; developing emerging talent and exporting it the rest of the UK; public engagement; and helping turn discoveries into treatments as quickly as possible to improve lives and strengthen the economy.

job_title: Software Development Engineer in Test - SDET / Automation Architect
job_description:
Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) / Automation Architect that has a very strong development background with ‘hands on’ experience of Test Automation, Continuous Integration and DevOps practices is required to re-locate and work overseas in Australia for a well-established and successful Company.
	

SALARY: $100,000 - $170,000 AUD per annum (equivalent to £56,000 - £95,500 per annum) + Visa & Relocation Package + Benefits

IMPORTANT - LOCATION: This job is based in Australia - The Company is looking for UK based candidates who want to relocate and work overseas in Australia - Visa Sponsorship and Relocation Package Available.

JOB TYPE: Full-Time, Permanent


** Relocate to Australia with VISA Sponsorship and Relocation Package **

** Exceptional Career Development Opportunities **

** Diverse Range of Projects ** 

** Clear Progression Path **

** Work for a Global, Dynamic, Market Leader **


KEY REQUIREMENTS: You must have a solid understanding of OOP development practices and with Java, C# or similar. You will also need extensive experience delivering high quality test automation solutions with tools such as Selenium, Ranorex, Coded UI etc.


JOB OVERVIEW

•	Are you a ‘hands on’ technical leader wanting to take your career to the next level?  

•	Would you like to relocate to sunny Australia with a Visa & Relocation package included?

•	Do you have the ability lead from the front within a dedicated technical division with a global leader?

•	Can you remain hands-on whilst working on strategic client engagements nationally?

If so, look no further...

We have a fantastic new job opportunity for a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) / Automation Architect that has a very strong development background with ‘hands on’ experience of Test Automation, Continuous Integration and DevOps practices.

Working as the Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) / Automation Architect you will be an automation evangelist and a top gun developer who knows how to build highly scalable, robust and maintainable solutions and lead successful teams and projects.

You will also be equally comfortable discussing best practice with developers and potential opportunities with clients. You must know how to assess risk and ROI and have the technical expertise to fix complex issues and architect high-quality solutions.

As the Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) / Automation Architect you will have a proven track record and the battle scars to prove it, enabling you to avoid common pitfalls and to mentor junior members of the team in best practice.

For the right person, the Company will provide a Visa, a competitive salary, a great working environment, training, mentorship and the support you need to advance your skills and experience, whilst developing a rewarding career.

If you’ve enjoyed the UK summer this year and would like over 200 days a year of sunshine, then Australia could be the place for you.


ABOUT THE COMPANY

As a successful candidate, you will have a great opportunity to join this well-established, market leading Company that employs over 1,500+ permanent employees globally and are currently experiencing rapid growth. Due to this rapid expansion, they are seeking highly experienced professional to join their Australian teams on a permanent basis. You will be working with the best in the industry and enjoying a variety of employee incentives including award-winning career development programmes.

The Company has offices in key cities throughout Australia, including Sydney, Brisbane, Perth and Melbourne.


APPLY TODAY

If this job looks like your ideal position then please send in your CV as soon as possible for our Recruitment Team to review.


DUTIES

Your duties and responsibilities as the Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) / Automation Architect include:

•	Define strategies and implementation plans which demonstrate ROI

•	Work with developers and test engineers to improve test and release processes

•	Build and maintain test tooling and frameworks

•	Manage stakeholder expectations and communicate with developers

•	Document and report progress, risks, issues and defects

•	Lead by example and demonstrate best practices in software development


IDEAL CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

•	A solid understanding of OOP development practices and with Java, C# or similar

•	Extensive experience delivering high quality test automation solutions with tools such as Selenium, Ranorex, Coded UI etc.

•	Demonstrated ability to solve technical problems and implement innovative solutions

•	Hands on experience with Continuous Integration tools such as Jenkins, Bamboo, Team City, Circle CI etc.

•	Web Service and API testing

•	BDD with tools such as Cucumber, SpecFlow, JBehave

•	Version control with GIT and a good understanding of branching and merging

•	A good understanding of web technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript and AJAX

•	Excellent relationship building and communication skills
 
Desirable Skills 

•	Cloud experience - AWS or Azure

•	Configuration Management tools such as Ansible, Chef, Puppet, OpsWorks etc.

•	Service Virtualisation / API Stubbing

•	Mobile Testing

•	Practical SQL and database experience


HOW TO APPLY

CV’s of Job Applicants meeting this requirement will be submitted to our Client for consideration. At the Home Office, we are working towards the common goals of keeping the British public safe and enhancing the nation’s wellbeing. Our work is high-profile, fast-paced, and offers access to the types of large-scale projects that cannot be matched elsewhere.  Tasked with controlling immigration, cutting crime, preventing terrorism and promoting economic growth, our people work across challenges that range from tackling anti-social behaviour and drug use, to exploring innovative technologies that will support policing capabilities. 

We place great value in the contribution that our Business Analysts make to the work of the Home Office, particularly in relation to the important change programmes that are currently underway. These programmes are addressing new technology and emerging threats, so their success is imperative.  When you join project delivery your work will therefore be felt across global issues, on large-scale projects that are career-defining - and that have the potential to impact on millions of lives. As such, you will work across multiple challenges, using a variety of methodologies in order to support the delivery of complex projects. Challenging yourself to find solutions to complex problems, you will enable the important transition from current business models to future ways of working, with your recommendations transforming project achievements. 

To excel in Home Office project delivery, you will have experience in a business analysis role, with the capacity to work in rapidly changing, agile environments. You will need excellent communication skills, with the ability to understand the requirements of multiple stakeholders. Using your understanding of process modelling, gap analysis and impact analysis, you will be involved in every element of these projects - so you will also need a good grasp of quality, risk and issue management. Crucially, you will have great people skills, motivating results and inspiring change. We embrace an inclusive and diverse working environment, where applications are invited from all backgrounds, so if you have skills that you believe are transferrable to this important field, then we would welcome your application. 

The Home Office provides a working environment that is as stimulating as it is supportive. With flexible working options (including the chance to apply for this position in a full-time, part-time or job-share capacity), we will help you achieve a good work/life balance. This will be further enhanced by a desirable benefits package including a generous pension, 34 days of holiday inclusive of bank holidays, plus one extra day of leave for the Queen’s birthday. Our people are encouraged to pursue their ambitions, so you will also have access to plenty of training and development opportunities.   

To work in a project where every day is different, and every challenge has the scope to enhance and improve the wellbeing of the British population, then get in touch today.   

To apply for this role please click the APPLY button.

DESCRIPTION

Lack of energy access in sub-Saharan Africa is a significant problem, with more than 600 million people currently without mains electricity supply. In Rwanda, 9.5 million people or 81% of the population cannot access mains electricity, which has a negative impact on quality of life and limits people’s chances of achieving economic prosperity.

BBOXX is helping to address this issue by providing off-grid communities in developing countries with affordable energy solutions. BBOXX customers typically use a Pay-As-You-Go payment method, which enables very low-income customers access to electricity at home that they would otherwise not be able to afford. To date BBOXX has installed more than 150,000 systems and provided around 750,000 people with access to energy.

Role Purpose: 

As an Embedded Software Engineer at BBOXX you will be based in our London office, where you will work alongside a growing team of Software Developers, Hardware Designers and Designers to develop the next generation of BBOXX products. Your role will be to create firmware solutions to monitor, control and remote update our off grid systems. You may also be working on the ecosystem around the products such as desktop tools used for diagnosing systems issues in our repair centres and production facilities.

Our Embedded Solutions:

STM32 family MCU's running C language based RTOS

Data driven and network connected using GSM and BT

About the role:

In a typical week you will be:

-Designing and Developing embedded solutions for products across multiple business segments
-Creating highly tested and production ready firmware releases
-Collaborating and problem solving with Electronic Engineering team members
-Creating tests and performing peer code reviews to increase quality
-Documenting domain specific items and reporting issues to relevant teams
-Contributing to continuous improvements in the team’s ways of working

You may also be:

Collaborating with other production and repair teams to increase overall productivity and in field reliability
Write scripts and tools to diagnose and log issues for both internal and external use

REQUIREMENTS

You should have all of:

-3+ years working experience in C for embedded systems
-Proven ability to design, develop and debug software for resource constrained systems
-Understanding of in-system communications (UART, I2C, SPI) and how to monitor (scope) them
-Familiarity with RTOS based development
-Working knowledge of IoT protocols such as CoAP, MQTT
-Ability to read and understand schematics, especially for low power electronics
-Some experience working with wireless communications technologies such as GSM or Bluetooth
--Understanding of ARM based microcontrollers such as STM32 family
-Good understanding of design patterns, software tools (Git) and modern develoment practices (design for test)
-Strong ownership ethics and ability to collaborate with others

And ideally some of:

-Experience of development for large volume consumser electronics devices
-Understanding and experience of AWS/Azure IoT platforms
-Experience supporting mass production electronics
-Understanding of testing processes and tools for embedded systems
-Proficiency in Python development

BENEFITS

-Competitive Salary based on experience
-Free breakfasts on Monday morning plus fresh fruit, hot drinks and biscuits every day.
-Working in BBOXX's start up culture - fast pace of development, lots of responsibility, opportunities to learn and implement your own ideas.
-Regular socials - BBOXX organises once monthly social events which can be proposed by anyone on the team. This could be anything from a picnic in the park to pottery painting or the regular pub quiz.

BBOXX will cover the cost of appropriate training courses to help you develop and learn new skills relevant for your role.

TECHNICAL IT PROGRAMME MANAGER

Zodiac Maritime is the largest privately-owned ship management company in the UK with a world-wide trading presence and recognised reputation for performance excellence.

Zodiac is headquartered in London with representative offices in New York, Shanghai, Japan and India, as well as 19 crew recruitment offices located around the world. We are currently in the early stages of our digital transformation journey. We are therefore recruiting for a hands on permanent Technical IT Programme Manager, who will be part of a small IT team reporting into the CIO.

Definition of Success in this Role:

We require a Technical IT Programme Manager who is a SME in O365 migration programmes:

- Experienced in working with our vendor partners in the successful setup, data migration, transition and decommissioning from our legacy systems to Office 365 (email migration and document management).
- Project delivery and implementation of group wide cyber security programmes and other identified improvement activities and digital transformation projects; on time, on budget and within scope.

- Recognised credibility across the organisation through a hands-on, collaborative approach, by bringing technical and commercial expertise, as well as a global future orientated perspective on technology, systems and infrastructure.
- Team collaboration and culture of continuous learning and service delivery.

More about You:

- Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or equivalent.
- Have 5 - 10 years' experience as a professional Project Manager. Ideally, full project life-cycle tenure accountability.
- Demonstrated experience in project planning, communication, dashboards, KPI's and PPM tools.

- Strong interpersonal skills; perceptive, personable, culturally-sensitive.
- Demonstrated a high degree if emotional intelligence.
- Ability to work collaboratively is essential; as is the ability to manage and nurture varied, senior stakeholder relationships.
- Possess excellent facilitation, collaboration and presentation skills.

We’re more than 459,000 people working in over 120 countries around the globe. Accenture Break|Through is a London-based, 10-week paid internship programme for returning managers starting in mid-January 2019. It’s specifically designed to provide a platform for experienced technology professionals to return to the workplace after a career break.

Throughout your Break|Through experience, we’ll ensure you have all the support you need with an experienced mentor who will be on-hand throughout to offer guidance and support. Bring your talent and passion to a global organisation at the forefront of innovation, and look ahead to an exceptional career in technology.
